terms.definition: The instantaneous rate of change of a function at a point, defined as the limit of average rates of change.
A function is differentiable at x if its derivative exists and is finite at that point.
The line through (x, f(x)) with slope f′(x); best linear approximation to the curve at x.
A point where f′(x) = 0 or f′ is undefined; candidate for local maxima, minima or inflection.
